$comment: Registry of structures we've built in the world, with coordinates. Append new builds here. Coordinates are Minecraft block coords (x, y, z).

builds.name: Grand Castle
builds.replaces: the old Spawn Castle + Moat + Drawbridge (erased; backed up to x+1500 / x1693-1736 via clone before clearing)
builds.status: first + second floor done (master bedrooms), fully torch-lit, AND walled grounds (curtain wall + 4 watchtowers + gatehouse + courtyard + giant king statue). Basement TBD.

layout.main_hall: central, x224-240 z100-142, TWO STORIES (y67-82). Iron throne (iron_block + lightning-rod sword spikes + iron_bars) on a blackstone dais at the north (z138-141) under a big stained-glass window; clerestory stained glass high on the E/W walls; chiseled-stone columns w/ lanterns; red-carpet runner; chandeliers; grand dark-oak entrance (south z100); two grand staircases up to the 2nd-floor balconies (y75).
layout.west_wing: 1 story (y67-74), x200-224: BALLROOM (front, quartz floor + chandeliers + musicians' dais), BARRACKS, ARMORY (anvil/smithing/armor stands), restroom (WC). Door from the hall at x224 z118.
layout.east_wing: 1 story, x240-264: DINING HALL (front, long table + chairs + chandeliers), KITCHEN (smoker/furnace/counters), SERVANT QUARTERS, STORAGE, restroom (WC). Door from the hall at x240 z118.
layout.second_floor: over both wings, floor y75 / walls y76-82 / deepslate roof y83 with parapets. LORDS CHAMBER (west, x201-223) and LADYS CHAMBER (east, x241-263): each a master suite with a four-poster canopy bed, brick fireplace (campfire), wardrobe/bookshelf, rug, hanging lantern, glass windows, wall torches. Reached via the grand stairs onto two balconies (x225-226 / x238-239) that overlook the great hall (iron-bar railings), then spruce doors through x224/x240 at z119.
layout.towers: 4 battlemented corner towers (7x7) to y88, with arrow slits
layout.lighting: wall torches throughout every 1st- and 2nd-floor room + towers (hall lit at two heights) so the whole interior is illuminated / mob-free
layout.grounds: Expanded+leveled plot (filled to y66 grass) enclosed by a CURTAIN WALL: outer faces S z70 / N z160 / W x170 / E x294, 6 blocks thick = a 4-wide wall-walk (y74) with crenellated battlements. GATEHOUSE on the south/castle axis (twin piers x224-228 & x236-240 to y82, archway passage x229-235 with raised iron-bar portcullis + lantern, wall-walk bridges over it). 4 WATCHTOWERS (9x9, y67-85) on the wall corners, each with a ground-level spruce door into the courtyard, the wall-walk passing in at y74 (wall-level entrance), an internal ladder, and a battlemented lookout roof. COURTYARD: polished-andesite approach paths framing the centerpiece, a fenced TRAINING YARD (coarse dirt, 6 armor-stand dummies in iron armor + hay/target backstops) in the east front, and 8 oak trees. GIANT KING STATUE centerpiece (smooth-quartz marble, ~32 tall) on a stone pedestal at x226-238 z80-90 facing the gate: robe, cape, gold belt/crown with red jewels, a glowing end-rod scepter + orb.

builds.name: Market Fair
builds.note: On expanded+leveled land OUTSIDE the south gate (z<70). Kept x<=258 to avoid the Looping Rollercoaster (x261-309, z29-61).
features: Main avenue (dirt_path + cobble) from the castle gate (x232) south to a WELCOME arch (z39) with bunting + banners; central WELL (x232 z41) with a roofed well-house.

8 merchant STALLS lining the avenue (striped wool awnings, counters w/ goods, workstation per trade, hanging lanterns): BAKERY(farmer), BUTCHERY(butcher), SMITHY(weaponsmith), APOTHECARY(cleric) on the west; FISHMONGER(fisherman), BOOKSELLER(librarian), WEAVERY(shepherd), TOOLERY(toolsmith) on the east. Each merchant is a NoAI profession villager.
LIVESTOCK pen (west, x182-204) fenced w/ trough+feed: 3 cows, 3 pigs, 4 chickens, 5 sheep + a farmer.
THE TAVERN (east, x246-258): open-front spruce hall, keg-barrel bar, tables/stools, cook + patron.
FORTUNE-TELLER tent (x211, purple/magenta wool peak) w/ amethyst+sea-lantern crystal ball + a cleric.
Performers' STAGE (x247 z60) raised deck w/ banner backdrop + note blocks + a performer.
Lamp posts + hay bales + flower pots + ~6 NoAI 'crowd' villagers. 19 villagers + 15 animals total.

builds.name: Grand Tower (apartments)
builds.footprint: 33x33, centered x232 z215, on a leveled district north of the castle (x198-266 z165-253). ~250 blocks tall (y66 base slab -> y316 roof), near the y319 build limit.
builds.design: ONE repeated floor blueprint stacked 50x (floor height 5: slab + 4 interior). Each floor = 4 big apartments (red/blue/lime/yellow beds + carpet + kitchenette) around a cross-corridor, with oak doors, hanging-lantern lighting (no mob spawns), and a full glass curtain wall with concrete mullions. Floor f slab at y=66+5f.
builds.elevator: 2x2 SCAFFOLDING express shaft at x232-233 z215-216, ground (y67) to roof (y316). Climb: hold jump to go up, sneak to descend. Each floor slab has a 2x2 hole for it (ground floor solid).
builds.extras: Ground LOBBY with entrance doors + 'GRAND TOWER / 50 FLOORS' sign on the south wall (z199); rooftop deck with parapet + sea-lantern lights + a tall iron-bar antenna mast topped with a light at y319.
builds.residents: tower_residents.py adds, per floor, two glowing yellow 'FLOOR N' signs flanking the elevator (on the z213 wall facing=south @ x231, and z217 wall facing=north @ x234, at y+2), and randomly populates ~55% of apartments with NoAI residents: 146 villagers (random profession + biome type) and 28 cats. NoAI so they never wander into the open elevator shaft. Idempotent (clears tower villagers/cats first; random.seed=1337).
builds.note: Built programmatically over RCON in ~1.2s. To change height: edit NFLOORS (then re-run tower_residents.py for the new floors). To climb faster than scaffolding, a soul-sand/water bubble elevator could replace the shaft.

builds.name: Family House
builds.backyard: Fenced yard NORTH of the house (x199-215 z165-177) reached by a new back door (z178): polished-andesite patio w/ table+chairs and a BBQ, a campfire fire pit w/ log-stump seats, a kids' play corner (sandbox + an A-frame swing), a doghouse with a dog named Rex, a garden shed (chest/tools), an oak tree, flowers and lantern lamp posts. North gate opens onto a leveled lawn strip toward the castle.
builds.mailbox: Curbside by the front gate (x208 z210): spruce post + barrel bin + a red-banner flag + an address sign, on a gravel curb.
builds.GOTCHA: This server has NO minecraft:chain block (setblock 'chain' errors 'Unknown block type'). Use iron_bars for ropes/hangers. Lanterns placed floating (expecting a chain above) have no support and pop on a block update — give them lantern[hanging=true] under a ceiling or a post below. Some older chandelier lanterns (castle/house/fair) may be missing for this reason.
builds.garden: Fenced front yard (south, x199-215 z195-209; spruce fence, south gate on the door axis, gravel+dirt-path walk to the front door). West = vegetable patch (hydrated farmland w/ grown wheat/carrots/potatoes/beetroot + a scarecrow). East = flower garden with a cherry tree (bee nest + 2 bees), a small pond w/ lily pads & sugar cane, scattered flowers, a bench and berry bushes. Lantern lamp posts.
builds.note: Built at player P_____L's location (~202,66,191), in the leveled district just west of the Grand Tower. 17x17 footprint, glass windows w/ timber mullions, gabled dark-oak roof + brick chimney. A 2-wide spruce staircase links every level (NW corner up; a 2nd flight by the east wall goes down to the basement).

levels.basement (y60): TREASURE VAULT: 6 auto-filling loot chests (buried_treasure, simple_dungeon, end_city_treasure, woodland_mansion, stronghold_corridor), a gold/emerald/diamond/amethyst hoard, an enchanting nook w/ bookshelves + anvil + brewing stand, a barrel wine cellar, cobwebs, lanterns.
levels.1F (y66): LIVING ROOM: brick fireplace w/ chimney, red-wool sofa + stairs facing it, rug + coffee table, bookshelves, a dining nook and a kitchen counter (furnace/smoker/cauldron). Spruce front door (south, z194) + stoop.
levels.2F (y72): 4 BEDROOMS off a central hallway: PARENTS (blue, NW), BOY (light-blue, NE), GIRL (pink, SW), GUEST (white, SE). Each: bed, nightstand+lantern, wardrobe barrel, carpet, bookshelf, ceiling lamp, name sign.
levels.3F (y78): STUDY: walls of bookshelves, a long desk w/ 3 lecterns + chairs, a reading rug w/ armchair, cartography + fletching tables.
levels.attic (y84): random junk under the gabled roof: mineshaft loot chests, barrels, hay, cobwebs, potted dead bush/fern, a gable window each end.

builds.name: Castle Guards (datapack)
builds.summary: Auto-respawning IRON-GOLEM guards that fight off monsters in/near the castle. A self-scheduling function keeps up to 6 golems (tag=castle_guard, named 'Castle Guard') alive, re-mustering at 4 courtyard posts (216/88, 248/88, 232/150, 196/120) whenever one dies — but only while a player is within the castle region (x160-310 z55-180) so it never over-spawns into unloaded chunks. Golems ignore the player + villagers and attack hostiles.

functions.guards:init: on world load (#minecraft:load) — creates the 'guards' scoreboard, sets cap=6, starts the patrol loop. Also the manual 'turn back on' command.
functions.guards:patrol: loops every 100t; runs maintain only if a player is in-region, then reschedules itself.
functions.guards:maintain: counts living guards in-region; if < cap, calls muster.
functions.guards:muster: summons one golem at the next of 4 rotating posts.
functions.guards:reset: kill all guards + reset rotation (they re-muster on the next tick).

builds.tune: Change the cap with: scoreboard players set #cap guards <n>. pack_format uses min_format[4,0]/max_format[9999,0] to match the working deathstar packs.

builds.name: Looping Rollercoaster
features: rideable powered rectangular circuit at y80 (powered_rail straights + curved corners), x265-305 / z33-57
GIANT decorative vertical loop (radius 12, ~24 tall, two red-concrete rails + white ties) straddling the front straight at z33, on a white A-frame
themed red/white support pillars on a gray-concrete plaza (ground y62); sea-lantern lighting
station platform + sign + a minecart on the back straight (z57)
builds.ride_notes: Vanilla rails can't ride a true vertical loop (the big loop is a showpiece). The circuit IS rideable: a stationary cart on a flat closed loop won't self-launch, so LEFT-CLICK (hit) the cart once to start it, then it circles on the powered track; right-click to board as it passes. (A button-launch spur is a possible upgrade.)

builds.name: Death Star
builds.status: exterior shell + iconic details (superlaser dish, equatorial trench, hull paneling)
builds.spawn: Whole-structure respawn via the 'deathstar' datapack (clone-blueprint approach; Java has no /structure save). 'function deathstar:capture' snapshots the live build into a hidden cold-storage copy at x+500; 'function deathstar:spawn' rematerializes it at the ORIGINAL coords (gate uses absolute coords, so v1 is origin-only per Decision B), re-parks the mech, and runs dsgate:install. Tiled into 34 y-slabs of 101x101x3 (<=32768 clone cap); one-tick lag spike on spawn. Verified 2026-06-20: 80/80 air-solid match, marker-revert restore, mech+gate OK.

superlaser_dish.notes: Concave bowl, ~48 across, iron/light-gray concentric rings, black core, sea-lantern focusing lens.

equatorial_trench.black_belt: the trench back wall (y138-142) is a black_concrete 'belt'. The backrooms reskin had clobbered most of it to yellow_terracotta (its replace-filter box reached the shell at the equator); restored 2026-06-20 via ds_belt_restore.py (per-angle inward march recolors only the outermost shell run -> black, leaving the interior maze yellow). Iron rims at y137/y143.
equatorial_trench.notes: Recessed channel around the whole sphere at the equator; black-concrete back wall (belt), iron rim shelves.

details.hull_paneling: 10 light-gray latitude seam rings (iron recoloured) for the paneled look

hangar.notes: REBUILT (ds_hangar.py): the maze that had capped the bay was cleared from x133..163, y120 up to the dome, restoring a tall domed bay (open to ~y155+). Clean gray-concrete deck at y118, flush floor light grid, two yellow/black landing pads (148,100 and 148,124), dome lights, and a glass-fronted control booth at the back-left (x134-139). A scaled-up Jaeger mech is parked at (150,119,112) facing the gate. Bulkhead (x132 + FACILITY door), the blast-door gate (x165) and its trigger plates (x162/x167) were left fully intact.

gate.type: bi-parting blast doors (iron), animated, no mods
gate.mechanism: vanilla datapack 'deathstar_gate' (functions dsgate:*), frame animation on a tick loop
gate.trigger: stone pressure plates inside (x162) and outside (x167) the bay; auto-opens on approach from either side, auto-closes ~5s after you leave (won't close while you're standing in it)
gate.status: WORKING (fixed 2026-06-20). The 1.21.11 server (datapack format 94.1) rejected the old pack.mcmeta (pack_format+supported_formats) — format >81 now REQUIRES min_format/max_format. Updated pack.mcmeta to {min_format:[4,0],max_format:[9999,0]}. NOTE: the special #minecraft:tick tag only re-attaches to the server loop on a full RESTART, not /reload — restart after editing datapacks. Run 'function dsgate:install' once if the dsgate scoreboard objective is ever missing.
gate.forcefield: passable blue particle shimmer (minecraft:dust, force mode) across the opening whenever the bay is open — fly through it like the magnetic field
gate.klaxon: 5-beep woo-wee note_block.bass alarm that sounds when the gate cycles open or closed
gate.source: minecraft/datapacks/deathstar_gate (deployed to world/datapacks on the mini)

facility_interior.separation: the y118 deck + a curved bulkhead at x132 separate the hangar (above) from the facility; main entry is an auto iron-door (signed 'FACILITY') at x132
facility_interior.elevator: central ladder shaft at (120,112) linking all decks; each deck has a foyer with an auto iron-door gate (stone pressure plates)
facility_interior.spiral_stair: stone-brick spiral staircase at (120,100), y104-143, around a lit central newel with iron-bar railings; links all decks (8 steps/revolution)
facility_interior.maze_status: FROZEN — the recursive-backtracker maze still fills each facility-deck donut, but the generator script is ABANDONED: every run did a full clear-pass that wiped the foyers/rooms/hangar. Do NOT regenerate it. All further edits are additive and confined to defined boxes.
facility_interior.layout: per-deck topology (exactly): ladder room --DoorA--> named facility room --DoorB--> maze. Each facility room is a fully enclosed box at x100..114 / z105..119 (interior x101..113, z106..118), so it's cleanly separated from the surrounding maze. DoorA (east wall, x114) opens onto a carved foyer (x115..119) leading to the ladder at x120; DoorB (west wall, x100) opens onto a short stub (x96..99) that breaks into the maze. Both are auto iron-doors (stone pressure plates).
facility_interior.spiral_landings: radius-4 landing rings at each deck level so you can step off the spiral onto the deck (treads at radius 3, untouched).
facility_interior.room_access: each named room is reached DIRECTLY from the ladder foyer (DoorA) and is never lost in the maze; DoorB is the only way onward into the maze. Built by minecraft/scripts/ds_rooms.py (additive, bounded).

decks.y104: Command Center (west) + living cluster off the ladder room: GAMING ROOM (east), BARRACKS (south), KITCHEN+CAFE (south-east). Same as y110, built via 'ds_living.py 104'.
decks.y110: Living quarters hub — off the ladder room: Living Room (west), GAMING ROOM (east: arcade/jukebox/note-blocks, screen wall, table, seats), BARRACKS (south: bunk rows + footlockers), KITCHEN+CAFE (south-east: counter w/ smoker/furnace/cauldron, bistro tables). Built additively by ds_living.py; the east corridor's backing column re-holds the elevator ladder (do not carve x121,z112).
decks.y118: Cafe + kitchen — counter with smoker/furnace/cauldron/barrels, bistro tables with chairs; same deck as the hangar bulkhead access
decks.y126: Restrooms — quartz/glazed-terracotta, stalls with doors + cauldron toilets, sink counter + glass mirrors
decks.y134: Conference Room — long blackstone boardroom table, chairs both sides + head chair, blue holo screen wall
decks.y142: Swimming Pool — recessed prismarine basin with water, dark-prismarine deck, pool ladder, quartz loungers, sea-lantern lighting

builds.notes: Giant hollow iron sphere, ~101 blocks across, 1-block hole-free shell. Floats west of spawn, clear of the castle. tp drops you inside the hollow interior.

entities.name: Battle Station (flying)
entities.notes: Pilotable flying station (arsenal mod). Right-click to board; W+look to fly (drifts toward aim), A/D strafe; no gravity / hovers; R fires the superlaser (orbital crater). Scale 6, 1000 HP. Placeholder iron-golem model for now (custom sphere model = Phase 3). /deathray <x y z> also exists as a standalone op command.

entities.name: Mech (Jaeger)
entities.mod: arsenal (folded in from the retired mech-mod 2026-06-20; old id mech:mech is gone)
entities.controls: right-click to pilot; WASD walk + mouse steer; sprint to charge; R fires the plasma cannon
entities.model: custom GeckoLib 5.4.5 humanoid Jaeger (legs/torso/pauldrons/cockpit head/arms) with idle+walk animations, JiJ-bundled; replaced the iron-golem placeholder. Geometry/texture are a first pass (refine in Blockbench).
entities.notes: Rideable IronGolem-based Jaeger from mech-mod, scaled to ~9-10 blocks via minecraft:scale (model + hitbox). HP 400, step height 3.0 (stomps over walls), speed 0.45, never despawns. Combat: sprint-charge (1.6x), CRUSH (damages+hurls entities it walks into while moving), and a server-authoritative PLASMA CANNON on the R key (beam along look, 14 dmg + ignite, no block damage, ~15-tick cooldown). Armored: immune to fall/fire/lava/drown/cactus/falling-block/in-wall, but takes combat damage. Iron-golem model is still the placeholder visual (custom GeckoLib model is the next step). Needs the mod on BOTH client and server. Spawned on demand via /mech.
